basic_details.job_title: Indian Air Force AFCAT 02/2026 Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online
basic_details.organization_name: Indian Air Force (IAF), Ministry of Defence, Govt. of India
basic_details.post_name: Commissioned Officer (Flying Branch & Ground Duty – Technical and Non-Technical)
basic_details.short_information: Indian Air Force AFCAT 02/2026 Recruitment: Get the latest Indian Air Force recruitment notification for commissioned officer vacancies in Flying Branch and Ground Duty (Technical and Non-Technical) Branches commencing July 2027. Online applications open on 20th May 2026 (1100 Hrs) and close on 19th June 2026 (2330 Hrs). Apply through the official website https://afcat.cdac.in. You can also get Free Job Alert of "Upcoming Indian Air Force AFCAT 02/2026 Recruitment" notifications for freshers and experienced candidates. Also know the direct official link for IAF AFCAT 02/2026 apply online from

qualification.details: Details on physical/medical standards, educational qualifications, and the full selection procedure are available in the detailed notification on the website. Candidates are advised to visit the official AFCAT website for branch-wise educational qualification requirements.

salary_details.pay_scale: Flying Officer: Rs. 56,100 – 1,77,500/- (Level 10 as per 7th CPC) + MSP Rs. 15,500/-

selection_process: Stage 1 AFCAT Online Examination / NCC Special Entry / GATE Score Screening
Stage 2 Air Force Selection Board (AFSB) Interview (including Psychological Test, Group Tests, and Interview) at various AFSB centres
Stage 3 Medical Examination at Air Force Central Medical Establishment (AFCME) / Institute of Aviation Medicine (IAM)
Stage 4 Final Merit List and Joining Instructions

The selection process for IAF AFCAT 02/2026 is merit-based. Candidates registering for AFCAT entry will have to pay the examination fee and appear for the AFCAT Online Test conducted at various centres. Details of conduct of exam
and mode of payment are available in the notification.
Physical Conditioning: Candidates are advised to be physically fit when reporting for SSB. Aim to achieve an ability to run 01 mile (1.6 km) in 10 minutes
do 10 push-ups and 03 chin-ups. Prospective candidates are also advised to keep themselves in good physical condition by training at RFA which encompasses running
and other forms of physical training/conditioning. Mandatory tests are conducted during training.
how_to_apply: Interested candidates should log on to https://afcat.cdac.in to apply. No other means/mode of application shall be accepted. Candidates are required to possess a valid email ID and active mobile phone number throughout the selection process. Online registration will open on 20-05-2026 (1100 Hrs) and close on 19-06-2026 (2330 Hrs).

Candidates eligible for one or more entries are required to register separately for each entry. After applying online, the candidate is required to download the application slip generated by the system with a unique application number. Information filled in by candidates in the online application form will be considered final and no amendments will be allowed subsequently. Candidates should exercise due care while entering their date of birth.
Candidates must maintain their unique registered Email ID and mobile phone number active throughout the selection process.
PAN Card and account in SBI/Nationalised Bank is mandatory at the time of joining Air Force Academy.
Aadhaar Card is mandatory for registration.

Candidates must be unmarried at the time of commencement of the course. Marriage during the period of training shall result in discharge and liability to refund all expenditure incurred by the Government.

For Queries: Related to conduct of online examination, registration process, and admit cards — contact +91 9513252077 or E-Mail [email protected]. For queries regarding eligibility, allotment of AFSB centres, conduct of AFSB interview, merit list, and Joining Instructions — contact 011-23010231 Extn 7610 or Toll free number 1800-11-2448.
important_instructions: Only Indian Citizens as per Citizenship Act 1955 are eligible to apply. Candidates must be unmarried at the time of commencement of the course. Permanent body tattoos are not permitted on any other body part except on the inner face of forearms (elbow to wrist) and on the reverse side (palm/back dorsal) of both hands. Self-declaration certificate is mandatory for small innocuous tattoos that are not prejudicial to good order and military discipline. Use/possession of narcotics is banned. Candidates found using narcotics at any time during training will be debarred from joining the IAF. Information filled in online application is final — no amendments allowed subsequently. Exercise due care while entering date of birth. Travel Allowance (TA) to and fro for travelling to AFSB will be reimbursed at AC-III Tier/AC Chair Car or actual normal bus fare for first-time candidates. No TA is admissible if you have already appeared for the same type of entry at any of the Selection Boards. Candidates must furnish correct and active Saving Bank Account details (not SB Account like Sukanya Samridhi etc.) for transfer of TA claims. Candidates are advised to keep themselves regularly updated through the official AFCAT website regarding all selection process alerts. Selection in the Indian Air Force is fair and merit-based. Any attempt to influence the selection process at any stage or use of any unfair means like forgery, impersonation, or supplying false information is liable to lead to termination of candidature or initiation of service and legal action against the candidate. Terms and conditions given in the advertisement are only guidelines and are subject to change without notice.

faq.question: Q1. What is AFCAT 02/2026 and which posts are notified?
faq.answer: AFCAT 02/2026 is the Indian Air Force Common Admission Test for recruitment of Commissioned Officers in Flying Branch and Ground Duty (Technical & Non‑Technical) branches, including NCC Special Entry and GATE Score Entry for courses commencing in July 2027.

faq.question: Q2. What are the AFCAT 02/2026 application dates?
faq.answer: For AFCAT 02/2026, online registration opens on 20 May 2026 (11:00 hrs) and closes on 19 June 2026 (23:30 hrs), and applications must be submitted only through the official AFCAT website.

faq.question: Q3. What is the age limit for AFCAT 02/2026 Flying Branch?
faq.answer: For the Flying Branch, the age limit is 20 to 24 years as on 01 July 2027, with a relaxable upper age of 26 years for candidates holding a valid Commercial Pilot Licence issued by DGCA (India).

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for Ground Duty (Technical and Non‑Technical) in AFCAT 02/2026?
faq.answer: For Ground Duty (Technical and Non‑Technical) branches (including GATE Score Entry), the age limit is 20 to 26 years as on 01 July 2027 for both men and women candidates.

faq.question: Q5. What is the educational qualification required for AFCAT 02/2026 Flying Branch?
faq.answer: For the Flying Branch, candidates must be graduates with minimum prescribed marks and must have studied Physics and Mathematics at 10+2 level or possess a BE/B.Tech degree in any discipline from a recognized university.

faq.question: Q6. What is the AFCAT 02/2026 exam fee?
faq.answer: For AFCAT entry, candidates must pay a non‑refundable exam fee of Rs. 550/- plus GST, while NCC Special Entry and GATE Score Entry candidates are exempt from paying any examination fee.

faq.question: Q7. What is the pay scale of an AFCAT 02/2026 Flying Officer after commissioning?
faq.answer: On successful completion of training, an IAF Flying Officer receives a pay scale of Rs. 56,100 – 1,77,500 (Level 10 as per 7th CPC) plus Military Service Pay (MSP) of Rs. 15,500 per month, along with applicable allowances.

faq.question: Q8. Is AFCAT 02/2026 open for both men and women candidates?
faq.answer: Yes, unmarried male and female Indian citizens can apply for Short Service Commission in Flying Branch and Ground Duty branches, and for Permanent Commission wherever notified, subject to eligibility conditions.

faq.question: Q9. What is the selection process for AFCAT 02/2026?
faq.answer: The AFCAT 02/2026 selection process includes an online AFCAT written exam (for AFCAT entry), followed by AFSB interview (Psychological tests, Group Tests and Personal Interview), and a medical examination, after which a final merit list is prepared.

faq.question: Q10. How can I apply online for Indian Air Force AFCAT 02/2026?
faq.answer: Eligible candidates must apply online only through the official AFCAT portal afcat.cdac.in , using a valid email ID and mobile number, and carefully filling personal, educational and branch preference details before the last date
